You are working on the pinterest/ktlint repository.
The codebase is at version 0.50 (commit: 7d8a509f).

## Issue to Resolve

Missing whitespace before function body is inserted in wrong place in AST
Given code below:
```
class UampNotificationManager(
    private val context: Context,
    sessionToken: MediaSessionCompat.Token,
    notificationListener: PlayerNotificationManager.NotificationListener,
) {
    fun showNotificationForPlayer(player: Player){
        notificationManager.setPlayer(player)
    }
}
```
and `.editorconfig`:
```
root = true

[**/*.{kt,kts}]
ktlint_code_style = ktlint_official
ktlint_experimental = enabled
ktlint_standard = enabled
```
then ktlint `0.50.0` is not able to format the code correctly in 3 runs.

When linting the original code, the following is reported:
```
src/main/kotlin/UampNotificationManager.kt:6:50: Missing spacing before "{" (standard:curly-spacing)
src/main/kotlin/UampNotificationManager.kt:6:50: Expected a single white space before start of function body (standard:function-start-of-body-spacing)
src/main/kotlin/UampNotificationManager.kt:6:50: Expected a single space before body block (standard:function-signature)
12:11:35.739 [main] WARN com.pinterest.ktlint.cli.internal.KtlintCommandLine - Lint has found errors than can be autocorrected using 'ktlint --format'
```
So three different rules are reporting the same error.

When running format, the output is:
```
12:12:49.476 [pool-1-thread-1] WARN com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.KtLintRuleEngine - Format was not able to resolve all violations which (theoretically) can be autocorrected in file .../src/main/kotlin/UampNotificationManager.kt in 3 consecutive runs of format.
```
Also, the file is formatted correctly *and* no more errors remain despite that formatting reports that not all problems were solved.

The problem is caused by inserting the whitespace element in the wrong position in the AST. If ktlint is restarted, the file is parsed again and the whitespace is in the expected place in the AST and no more error is to be fixed.

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `ktlint-rule-engine-core/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/rule/engine/core/api/ASTNodeExtension.kt`
- `ktlint-rule-engine/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/rule/engine/api/KtLintRuleEngine.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/FunctionSignatureRule.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

173→                                // In trace mode report the violation immediately. The order in which violations are actually found might be
174→                                // different from the order in which they are reported. For debugging purposes it can be helpful to know the
175→                                // exact order in which violations are being solved.
176→                                LOGGER.trace { "Format violation: ${lintError.logMessage(code)}" }
177→                            }
178→                    }
179→                }
180→        } while (mutated && formatRunCount < MAX_FORMAT_RUNS_PER_FILE)
181→        if (formatRunCount == MAX_FORMAT_RUNS_PER_FILE && mutated) {
182→            LOGGER.warn {
183→                "Format was not able to resolve all violations which (theoretically) can be autocorrected in file " +
184→                    "${code.filePathOrStdin()} in $MAX_FORMAT_RUNS_PER_FILE consecutive runs of format."
185→            }
186→        }
187→
188→        errors
189→            .sortedWith { (l), (r) -> if (l.line != r.line) l.line - r.line else l.col - r.col }
190→            .forEach { (e, corrected) -> callback(e, corrected) }
191→
192→        if (!mutated && formatRunCount == 1) {
193→            return code.content
194→        }
195→
196→        val formattedCode =
197→            ruleExecutionContext
198→                .rootNode
199→                .text
200→                .replace("\n", ruleExecutionContext.determineLineSeparator(code.content))
201→        return if (hasUTF8BOM) {
202→            UTF8_BOM + formattedCode
203→        } else {
204→            formattedCode
205→        }.also {
206→            LOGGER.debug("Finished with formatting file '${code.fileNameOrStdin()}'")
207→        }
208→    }
209→
210→    private fun RuleExecutionContext.determineLineSeparator(fileContent: String): String {
211→        val eol = editorConfig[END_OF_LINE_PROPERTY]
212→        return when {
213→            eol == crlf || eol != lf && fileContent.lastIndexOf('\r') != -1 -> "\r\n"
214→            else -> "\n"
215→        }
216→    }
217→
218→    /**
219→     * Generates Kotlin `.editorconfig` file section content based on a path to a file or directory. Given that path, all '.editorconfig'
220→     * files on that path are taken into account to determine the values of properties which are already used.
221→     *
222→     * @return Kotlin section editorconfig content. For example:
223→     * ```properties
224→     * final-newline=true
225→     * indent-size=4
226→     * ```
227→     */
228→    public fun generateKotlinEditorConfigSection(filePath: Path): String {
229→        val codeStyle =
230→            editorConfigOverride
231→                .properties[CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY]
232→                ?.parsed
233→                ?.safeAs<CodeStyleValue>()
234→                ?: CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY.defaultValue
235→        val rules =
236→            ruleProviders
237→                .map { it.createNewRuleInstance() }
238→                .distinctBy { it.ruleId }
239→                .toSet()
240→        return EditorConfigGenerator(fileSystem, editorConfigLoaderEc4j)
241→            .generateEditorconfig(
242→                rules,
243→                codeStyle,
244→                filePath,
245→            )
246→    }
247→
248→    /**
249→     * Reduce memory usage by cleaning internal caches.
250→     */
251→    public fun trimMemory() {
252→        THREAD_SAFE_EDITOR_CONFIG_CACHE.clear()
253→    }
254→
255→    /**
256→     * Get the list of files which will be accessed by KtLint when linting or formatting the given file or directory.
257→     * The API consumer can use this list to observe changes in '.editorconfig' files. Whenever such a change is
258→     * observed, the API consumer should call [reloadEditorConfigFile].
259→     * To avoid unnecessary access to the file system, it is best to call this method only once for the root of the
260→     * project which is to be [lint] or [format].
261→     */
262→    public fun editorConfigFilePaths(path: Path): List<Path> = EditorConfigFinder(editorConfigLoaderEc4j).findEditorConfigs(path)
263→
264→    /**
265→     * Reloads an '.editorconfig' file given that it is currently loaded into the KtLint cache. This method is intended
266→     * to be called by the API consumer when it is aware of changes in the '.editorconfig' file that should be taken
267→     * into account with next calls to [lint] and/or [format]. See [editorConfigFilePaths] to get the list of
268→     * '.editorconfig' files which need to be observed.
269→     */
270→    public fun reloadEditorConfigFile(path: Path) {
271→        THREAD_SAFE_EDITOR_CONFIG_CACHE.reloadIfExists(
272→            Resource.Resources.ofPath(path, StandardCharsets.UTF_8),
273→        )
274→    }
275→
276→    public fun transformToAst(code: Code): FileASTNode = createRuleExecutionContext(this, code).rootNode
277→
278→    private fun LintError.logMessage(code: Code) =
279→        "${code.fileNameOrStdin()}:$line:$col: $detail ($ruleId)" +
280→            if (canBeAutoCorrected) {
281→                ""
282→            } else {
283→                " [cannot be autocorrected]"
284→            }
285→
286→    public companion object {
287→        internal const val UTF8_BOM = "\uFEFF"
288→
289→        public const val STDIN_FILE: String = "<stdin>"
290→
291→        private const val MAX_FORMAT_RUNS_PER_FILE = 3
292→    }
293→}

 52→
 53→public class FunctionSignatureRule :
 54→    StandardRule(
 55→        id = "function-signature",
 56→        visitorModifiers =
 57→            setOf(
 58→                // Run after wrapping and spacing rules
 59→                VisitorModifier.RunAsLateAsPossible,
 60→            ),
 61→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 62→            setOf(
 63→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 64→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 65→                MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY,
 66→                FORCE_MULTILINE_WHEN_PARAMETER_COUNT_GREATER_OR_EQUAL_THAN_PROPERTY,
 67→                FUNCTION_BODY_EXPRESSION_WRAPPING_PROPERTY,
 68→            ),
 69→    ),
 70→    Rule.Experimental {
 71→    private var codeStyle = CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 72→    private var indentConfig = DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 73→    private var maxLineLength = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 74→    private var functionSignatureWrappingMinimumParameters =
 75→        FORCE_MULTILINE_WHEN_PARAMETER_COUNT_GREATER_OR_EQUAL_THAN_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 76→    private var functionBodyExpressionWrapping = FUNCTION_BODY_EXPRESSION_WRAPPING_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 77→
 78→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 79→        codeStyle = editorConfig[CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY]
 80→        functionSignatureWrappingMinimumParameters = editorConfig[FORCE_MULTILINE_WHEN_PARAMETER_COUNT_GREATER_OR_EQUAL_THAN_PROPERTY]
 81→        functionBodyExpressionWrapping = editorConfig[FUNCTION_BODY_EXPRESSION_WRAPPING_PROPERTY]
 82→        indentConfig =
 83→            IndentConfig(
 84→                indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 85→                tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 86→            )
 87→        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
 88→    }
 89→
 90→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 91→        node: ASTNode,
 92→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 93→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 94→    ) {
 95→        if (node.elementType == FUN) {
 96→            node
 97→                .functionSignatureNodes()
 98→                .any { it.elementType == EOL_COMMENT || it.elementType == BLOCK_COMMENT }
 99→                .ifTrue {
100→                    // Rewriting function signatures in a consistent manner is hard or sometimes even impossible. For
101→                    // example a multiline signature which could fit on one line can not be rewritten in case it
102→                    // contains an EOL comment. Rewriting a single line signature which exceeds the max line length to a
103→                    // multiline signature is hard when it contains block comments. For now, it does not seem worth the
104→                    // effort to attempt it.
105→                    return
106→                }
107→
108→            visitFunctionSignature(node, emit, autoCorrect)
109→        }
110→    }
111→
112→    private fun ASTNode.functionSignatureNodes(): List<ASTNode> {
113→        // Find the signature including the element that has to be placed on the same line as the function signature
114→        //     fun foo(bar: String) {
115→        // or
116→        //     fun foo(bar: String) =
117→        val firstCodeChild = getFirstCodeChild()
118→        val startOfBodyBlock =
119→            this
120→                .findChildByType(BLOCK)
121→                ?.firstChildNode
122→        val startOfBodyExpression = this.findChildByType(EQ)
123→        return collectLeavesRecursively()
124→            .childrenBetween(
125→                startASTNodePredicate = { it == firstCodeChild },
126→                endASTNodePredicate = { it == startOfBodyBlock || it == startOfBodyExpression },
127→            )
128→    }
129→
130→    private fun ASTNode.getFirstCodeChild(): ASTNode? {
131→        val funNode =
132→            if (elementType == FUN_KEYWORD) {
133→                this.treeParent
134→            } else {
135→                this
136→            }
137→        funNode
138→            ?.findChildByType(MODIFIER_LIST)
139→            ?.let { modifierList ->
140→                val iterator = modifierList.children().iterator()
141→                var currentNode: ASTNode
142→                while (iterator.hasNext()) {
143→                    currentNode = iterator.next()
144→                    if (currentNode.elementType != ANNOTATION &&
145→                        currentNode.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY &&
146→                        currentNode.elementType != WHITE_SPACE
147→                    ) {
148→                        return currentNode
149→                    }
150→                }
151→                return modifierList.nextCodeSibling()
152→            }
153→        return funNode.nextCodeLeaf()
154→    }
155→
156→    private fun visitFunctionSignature(
157→        node: ASTNode,
158→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
159→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
160→    ) {
161→        require(node.elementType == FUN)
162→
163→        val forceMultilineSignature =
164→            node.hasMinimumNumberOfParameters() ||
165→                node.containsMultilineParameter() ||
166→                (codeStyle == ktlint_official && node.containsAnnotatedParameter())
167→        if (isMaxLineLengthSet()) {
168→            val singleLineFunctionSignatureLength = calculateFunctionSignatureLengthAsSingleLineSignature(node, emit, autoCorrect)
169→            // Function signatures not having parameters, should not be reformatted automatically. It would result in function signatures
170→            // like below, which are not acceptable:
171→            //     fun aVeryLongFunctionName(
172→            //     ) = "some-value"
173→            //
174→            //     fun aVeryLongFunctionName(
175→            //     ): SomeVeryLongTypeName =
176→            //         SomeVeryLongTypeName(...)
177→            // Leave it up to the max-line-length rule to detect those violations so that the developer can handle it manually.
178→            val rewriteFunctionSignatureWithParameters = node.countParameters() > 0 && singleLineFunctionSignatureLength > maxLineLength
179→            if (forceMultilineSignature || rewriteFunctionSignatureWithParameters) {
180→                fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline = true, dryRun = false)
181→                // Due to rewriting the function signature, the remaining length on the last line of the multiline
182→                // signature needs to be recalculated
183→                val lengthOfLastLine = recalculateRemainLengthForFirstLineOfBodyExpression(node)
184→                fixFunctionBody(node, emit, autoCorrect, maxLineLength - lengthOfLastLine)
185→            } else {
186→                fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline = false, dryRun = false)
187→                fixFunctionBody(node, emit, autoCorrect, maxLineLength - singleLineFunctionSignatureLength)
188→            }
189→        } else {
190→            // When max line length is not set then keep it as single line function signature only when the original
191→            // signature already was a single line signature. Otherwise, rewrite the entire signature as a multiline
192→            // signature.
193→            val rewriteToSingleLineFunctionSignature =
194→                node
195→                    .functionSignatureNodes()
196→                    .none { it.textContains('\n') }
197→            if (!forceMultilineSignature && rewriteToSingleLineFunctionSignature) {
198→                fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline = false, dryRun = false)
199→            } else {
200→                fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline = true, dryRun = false)
201→            }
202→        }
203→    }
204→
205→    private fun recalculateRemainLengthForFirstLineOfBodyExpression(node: ASTNode): Int {
206→        val closingParenthesis =
207→            node
208→                .findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST)
209→                ?.findChildByType(RPAR)
210→        val tailNodesOfFunctionSignature =
211→            node
212→                .functionSignatureNodes()
213→                .childrenBetween(
214→                    startASTNodePredicate = { it == closingParenthesis },
215→                    endASTNodePredicate = { false },
216→                )
217→
218→        return node.indent(false).length +
219→            tailNodesOfFunctionSignature.sumOf { it.text.length }
220→    }
221→
222→    private fun ASTNode.containsMultilineParameter(): Boolean =
223→        findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST)
224→            ?.children()
225→            .orEmpty()
226→            .filter { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
227→            .any { it.textContains('\n') }
228→
229→    private fun ASTNode.containsAnnotatedParameter(): Boolean =
230→        findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST)
231→            ?.children()
232→            .orEmpty()
233→            .filter { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
234→            .any { it.isAnnotated() }
235→
236→    private fun ASTNode.isAnnotated() =
237→        findChildByType(MODIFIER_LIST)
238→            ?.children()
239→            .orEmpty()
240→            .any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
241→
242→    private fun calculateFunctionSignatureLengthAsSingleLineSignature(
243→        node: ASTNode,
244→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
245→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
246→    ): Int {
247→        val actualFunctionSignatureLength = node.getFunctionSignatureLength()
248→
249→        // Calculate the length of the function signature in case it would be rewritten as single line (and without a
250→        // maximum line length). The white space correction will be calculated via a dry run of the actual fix.
251→        return actualFunctionSignatureLength +
252→            // Calculate the white space correction in case the signature would be rewritten to a single line
253→            fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline = false, dryRun = true)
254→    }
255→
256→    private fun ASTNode.getFunctionSignatureLength() = indent(false).length + getFunctionSignatureNodesLength()
257→
258→    private fun ASTNode.getFunctionSignatureNodesLength() =
259→        functionSignatureNodes()
260→            .joinTextToString()
261→            .length
262→
263→    private fun fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(
264→        node: ASTNode,
265→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
266→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
267→        multiline: Boolean,
268→        dryRun: Boolean,
269→    ): Int {
270→        var whiteSpaceCorrection = 0
271→
272→        val valueParameterList = requireNotNull(node.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST))
273→        val firstParameterInList =
274→            valueParameterList
275→                .children()
276→                .firstOrNull { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
277→
278→        whiteSpaceCorrection +=
279→            if (firstParameterInList == null) {
280→                // handle empty parameter list
281→                fixWhiteSpacesInEmptyValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, dryRun)
282→            } else {
283→                fixWhiteSpacesBeforeFirstParameterInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline, dryRun) +
284→                    fixWhiteSpacesBetweenParametersInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline, dryRun) +
285→                    fixWhiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline, dryRun)
286→            }
287→
288→        return whiteSpaceCorrection
289→    }
290→
291→    private fun fixWhiteSpacesInEmptyValueParameterList(
292→        node: ASTNode,
293→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
294→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
295→        dryRun: Boolean,
296→    ): Int {
297→        var whiteSpaceCorrection = 0
298→
299→        val valueParameterList = requireNotNull(node.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST))
300→
301→        valueParameterList
302→            .children()
303→            .filter { it.elementType != LPAR && it.elementType != RPAR }
304→            .also { elementsInValueParameterList ->
305→                // Functions with comments in the value parameter list are excluded from processing before. So an "empty" value
306→                // parameter list should only contain a single whitespace element
307→                require(elementsInValueParameterList.count() <= 1)
308→            }
309→            .firstOrNull()
310→            ?.let { whiteSpace ->
311→                if (!dryRun) {
312→                    emit(
313→                        whiteSpace.startOffset,
314→                        "No whitespace expected in empty parameter list",
315→                        true,
316→                    )
317→                }
318→                if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
319→                    whiteSpace.treeParent.removeChild(whiteSpace)
320→                } else {
321→                    whiteSpaceCorrection -= whiteSpace.textLength
322→                }
323→            }
324→
325→        return whiteSpaceCorrection
326→    }
327→
328→    private fun fixWhiteSpacesBeforeFirstParameterInValueParameterList(
329→        node: ASTNode,
330→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
331→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
332→        multiline: Boolean,
333→        dryRun: Boolean,
334→    ): Int {
335→        var whiteSpaceCorrection = 0
336→
337→        val valueParameterList = requireNotNull(node.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST))
338→        val firstParameterInList =
339→            valueParameterList
340→                .children()
341→                .first { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
342→
343→        val firstParameter = firstParameterInList.firstChildNode
344→        firstParameter
345→            ?.prevLeaf()
346→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE }
347→            .let { whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier ->
348→                if (multiline) {
349→                    val expectedParameterIndent = indentConfig.childIndentOf(node)
350→                    if (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier == null ||
351→                        whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier.text != expectedParameterIndent
352→                    ) {
353→                        if (!dryRun) {
354→                            emit(
355→                                firstParameterInList.startOffset,
356→                                "Newline expected after opening parenthesis",
357→                                true,
358→                            )
359→                        }
360→                        if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
361→                            valueParameterList.firstChildNode.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(expectedParameterIndent)
362→                        } else {
363→                            whiteSpaceCorrection += expectedParameterIndent.length - (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier?.textLength ?: 0)
364→                        }
365→                    }
366→                } else {
367→                    if (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier != null) {
368→                        if (!dryRun) {
369→                            emit(
370→                                firstParameter!!.startOffset,
371→                                "No whitespace expected between opening parenthesis and first parameter name",
372→                                true,
373→                            )
374→                        }
375→                        if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
376→                            whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier.treeParent.removeChild(whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier)
377→                        } else {
378→                            whiteSpaceCorrection -= whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier.textLength
379→                        }
380→                    }
381→                }
382→            }
383→
384→        return whiteSpaceCorrection
385→    }
386→
387→    private fun fixWhiteSpacesBetweenParametersInValueParameterList(
388→        node: ASTNode,
389→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
390→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
391→        multiline: Boolean,
392→        dryRun: Boolean,
393→    ): Int {
394→        var whiteSpaceCorrection = 0
395→
396→        val valueParameterList = requireNotNull(node.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST))
397→        val firstParameterInList =
398→            valueParameterList
399→                .children()
400→                .first { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
401→
402→        valueParameterList
403→            .children()
404→            .filter { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
405→            .filter { it != firstParameterInList }
406→            .forEach { valueParameter ->
407→                val firstChildNodeInValueParameter = valueParameter.firstChildNode
408→                firstChildNodeInValueParameter
409→                    ?.prevLeaf()
410→                    ?.takeIf { it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE }
411→                    .let { whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier ->
412→                        if (multiline) {
413→                            val expectedParameterIndent = indentConfig.childIndentOf(node)
414→                            if (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier == null ||
415→                                whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier.text != expectedParameterIndent
416→                            ) {
417→                                if (!dryRun) {
418→                                    emit(
419→                                        valueParameter.startOffset,
420→                                        "Parameter should start on a newline",
421→                                        true,
422→                                    )
423→                                }
424→                                if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
425→                                    firstChildNodeInValueParameter.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(expectedParameterIndent)
426→                                } else {
427→                                    whiteSpaceCorrection += expectedParameterIndent.length - (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier?.textLength ?: 0)
428→                                }
429→                            }
430→                        } else {
431→                            if (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier == null || whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier.text != " ") {
432→                                if (!dryRun) {
433→                                    emit(
434→                                        firstChildNodeInValueParameter!!.startOffset,
435→                                        "Single whitespace expected before parameter",
436→                                        true,
437→                                    )
438→                                }
439→                                if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
440→                                    firstChildNodeInValueParameter.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(" ")
441→                                } else {

content.thinking:
**Analyzing whitespace handling**

I think whitespace between composite elements should be siblings under their common parent, not inside the first composite. This makes me wonder if our methods `upsertWhitespaceAfterMe/BeforeMe` need special handling to avoid issues with `VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST`. When we call `.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe` on a leaf, we want the whitespace after the composite node instead. It seems I’m facing challenges where the whitespace gets inserted at the wrong position, but after a reparse, everything looks fine. So, it appears the insertion method is causing the structural mismatch.**Examining whitespace issues**

After formatting runs, I notice a warning because subsequent rules are traversing the AST. It seems that if whitespace is inserted inside `VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST` after `)`, the signature nodes may not include it when traversing. I'm realizing that `functionSignatureNodes` collects leaves up to the `startOfBodyBlock`. If the whitespace is inserted inside the parameter list, it might be included in the leaf order. The key issue is actually the missing spacing before the curly brace `{`, which is highlighted by multiple spacing checks.**Analyzing AST navigation and whitespace**

I think each rule might use different AST navigation methods. For example, `curly-spacing` seems to check the previous sibling of `LBRACE` and maybe expects a whitespace sibling in the FUN tree. However, if whitespace is inserted inside `VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST`, it won't be seen as a sibling of `BLOCK` or `LBRACE`, which can lead to repeated corrections. After a reparse, the whitespace ends up in the right place, but I need to ensure whitespace gets inserted correctly before the curly brace. I should consider fixing `upsertWhitespaceAfterMe` to handle these cases better.**Considering whitespace insertion logic**

I’m thinking that if the next node exists and is whitespace, I should replace it. If not, I’ll insert whitespace after the current leaf. For a `)` leaf, `treeNext` might be null, meaning there's no sibling in the list. It looks like `nextLeaf()` would return the next leaf in tree order, which could be the `{` leaf in BLOCK. Ideally, if there is no `treeNext` but a non-null `nextLeaf` in another subtree, I need to insert whitespace before that `nextLeaf` since it should belong to the common parent. I can check if `nextLeaf.treeParent` is different from `current.treeParent`.**Exploring whitespace insertion in AST**

I'm considering that inserting whitespace before the next leaf might actually work well. If I use `upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe` on the next leaf, it checks for the previous leaf. However, for the `{` leaf inside BLOCK, `treePrev` could be null. The `prevLeaf()` would return `)`, and inserting before that means whitespace gets placed as a child of BLOCK, which isn’t ideal since the correct placement is before BLOCK itself. Whitespace between the signature and body should be outside BLOCK, specifically between `)` and `{`, placing it as sibling nodes under the FUN parent. I’ll check how Kotlin PSI manages this whitespace organization—maybe a small test can help clarify!

 36→public class SpacingAroundCurlyRule : StandardRule("curly-spacing") {
 37→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 38→        node: ASTNode,
 39→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 40→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 41→    ) {
 42→        if (node.isLeaf() && !node.isPartOfString()) {
 43→            val prevLeaf = node.prevLeaf()
 44→            val nextLeaf = node.nextLeaf()
 45→            val spacingBefore: Boolean
 46→            val spacingAfter: Boolean
 47→            if (node.elementType == LBRACE) {
 48→                spacingBefore =
 49→                    prevLeaf is PsiWhiteSpace ||
 50→                    prevLeaf?.elementType == AT ||
 51→                    (
 52→                        prevLeaf?.elementType == LPAR &&
 53→                            ((node as LeafPsiElement).parent is KtLambdaExpression || node.parent.parent is KtLambdaExpression)
 54→                        )
 55→                spacingAfter = nextLeaf is PsiWhiteSpace || nextLeaf?.elementType == RBRACE
 56→                if (prevLeaf is PsiWhiteSpace &&
 57→                    !prevLeaf.textContains('\n') &&
 58→                    prevLeaf.prevLeaf()?.let {
 59→                        it.elementType == LPAR || it.elementType == AT
 60→                    } == true
 61→                ) {
 62→                    emit(node.startOffset, "Unexpected space before \"${node.text}\"", true)
 63→                    if (autoCorrect) {
 64→                        prevLeaf.node.treeParent.removeChild(prevLeaf.node)
 65→                    }
 66→                }
 67→                if (prevLeaf is PsiWhiteSpace && prevLeaf.textContains('\n') &&
 68→                    (
 69→                        prevLeaf.prevLeaf()?.let { it.elementType == RPAR || KtTokens.KEYWORDS.contains(it.elementType) } == true ||
 70→                            node.treeParent.elementType == CLASS_BODY ||
 71→                            (prevLeaf.treeParent.elementType == FUN && prevLeaf.treeNext.elementType != LAMBDA_EXPRESSION)
 72→                        ) // allow newline for lambda return type
 73→                ) {
 74→                    emit(node.startOffset, "Unexpected newline before \"${node.text}\"", true)
 75→                    if (autoCorrect) {
 76→                        val eolCommentExists =
 77→                            prevLeaf
 78→                                .prevLeaf()
 79→                                ?.let { it is PsiComment && it.elementType == EOL_COMMENT }
 80→                                ?: false
 81→                        if (eolCommentExists) {
 82→                            val commentLeaf = prevLeaf.prevLeaf()!!
 83→                            if (commentLeaf.prevLeaf() is PsiWhiteSpace) {
 84→                                (commentLeaf.prevLeaf() as LeafPsiElement).rawRemove()
 85→                            }
 86→                            (node.treeParent.treeParent as TreeElement).removeChild(commentLeaf)
 87→                            (node.treeParent as TreeElement).addChild(commentLeaf, node.treeNext)
 88→                            node.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(" ")
 89→                        }
 90→                        (prevLeaf as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(" ")
 91→                    }
 92→                }
 93→            } else if (node.elementType == RBRACE) {
 94→                spacingBefore = prevLeaf is PsiWhiteSpace || prevLeaf?.elementType == LBRACE
 95→                spacingAfter = nextLeaf == null || nextLeaf is PsiWhiteSpace || shouldNotToBeSeparatedBySpace(nextLeaf)
 96→                if (nextLeaf is PsiWhiteSpace && !nextLeaf.textContains('\n') &&
 97→                    shouldNotToBeSeparatedBySpace(nextLeaf.nextLeaf())
 98→                ) {
 99→                    emit(node.startOffset, "Unexpected space after \"${node.text}\"", true)
100→                    if (autoCorrect) {
101→                        nextLeaf.node.treeParent.removeChild(nextLeaf.node)
102→                    }
103→                }
104→            } else {
105→                return
106→            }
107→            when {
108→                !spacingBefore && !spacingAfter -> {
109→                    emit(node.startOffset, "Missing spacing around \"${node.text}\"", true)
110→                    if (autoCorrect) {
111→                        node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(" ")
112→                        node.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(" ")
113→                    }
114→                }
115→                !spacingBefore -> {
116→                    emit(node.startOffset, "Missing spacing before \"${node.text}\"", true)
117→                    if (autoCorrect) {
118→                        node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(" ")
119→                    }
120→                }
121→                !spacingAfter -> {
122→                    emit(node.startOffset + 1, "Missing spacing after \"${node.text}\"", true)
123→                    if (autoCorrect) {
124→                        node.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(" ")
125→                    }
126→                }
127→            }
128→        }
129→    }
130→
131→    private fun shouldNotToBeSeparatedBySpace(leaf: ASTNode?): Boolean {
132→        val nextElementType = leaf?.elementType
133→        return (
134→            nextElementType == DOT ||
135→                nextElementType == COMMA ||
136→                nextElementType == RBRACKET ||
137→                nextElementType == RPAR ||
138→                nextElementType == SEMICOLON ||
139→                nextElementType == SAFE_ACCESS ||
140→                nextElementType == EXCLEXCL ||
141→                nextElementType == LBRACKET ||
142→                nextElementType == LPAR ||
143→                nextElementType == COLONCOLON
144→            )
145→    }
146→}

import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.Code
import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.KtLintRuleEngine
import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.BLOCK
import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.FUN
import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.LBRACE
import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.RPAR
import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST
import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.WHITE_SPACE
import org.assertj.core.api.Assertions.assertThat
import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.FileASTNode
import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.PsiWhiteSpaceImpl
import org.junit.jupiter.api.Test

class ASTNodeExtensionWhitespaceInsertionTest {
    @Test
    fun `Given a function without whitespace before body then upsertWhitespaceAfterMe inserts the whitespace as sibling before the block`() {
        val code = "fun foo(){}"
        val root = transformCodeToAST(code)
        val funNode = root.findChildByType(FUN)!!
        val block = funNode.findChildByType(BLOCK)!!
        val valueParameterList = funNode.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST)!!
        val rpar = valueParameterList.findChildByType(RPAR)!!

        rpar.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(" ")

        val whitespaceBeforeBlock = block.prevLeaf(includeEmpty = true)
        assertThat(whitespaceBeforeBlock?.elementType).isEqualTo(WHITE_SPACE)
        assertThat(whitespaceBeforeBlock?.text).isEqualTo(" ")
        assertThat(block.firstChildNode.elementType).isEqualTo(LBRACE)
        assertThat(root.text).isEqualTo("fun foo() {}")
    }

    @Test
    fun `Given a function without whitespace before body then upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe on lbrace inserts the whitespace as sibling before the block`() {
        val code = "fun foo(){}"
        val root = transformCodeToAST(code)
        val funNode = root.findChildByType(FUN)!!
        val block = funNode.findChildByType(BLOCK)!!
        val lbrace = block.firstChildNode

        lbrace.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(" ")

        val whitespaceBeforeBlock = block.prevLeaf(includeEmpty = true)
        assertThat(whitespaceBeforeBlock?.elementType).isEqualTo(WHITE_SPACE)
        assertThat(whitespaceBeforeBlock?.text).isEqualTo(" ")
        assertThat(block.firstChildNode.elementType).isEqualTo(LBRACE)
        assertThat(root.text).isEqualTo("fun foo() {}")
    }

    @Test
    fun `Given whitespace inserted inside a block before lbrace then upsertWhitespaceAfterMe relocates it to the parent of the block`() {
        val code = "fun foo(){}"
        val root = transformCodeToAST(code)
        val funNode = root.findChildByType(FUN)!!
        val block = funNode.findChildByType(BLOCK)!!
        val valueParameterList = funNode.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST)!!
        val rpar = valueParameterList.findChildByType(RPAR)!!
        val lbrace = block.firstChildNode

        // Simulate old behaviour: whitespace added as first child of the block (before '{')
        block.addChild(PsiWhiteSpaceImpl(" "), lbrace)

        // Sanity check: the block is still not preceded by whitespace
        assertThat(block.prevLeaf(includeEmpty = true)?.elementType).isNotEqualTo(WHITE_SPACE)
        assertThat(block.firstChildNode.elementType).isEqualTo(WHITE_SPACE)

        rpar.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(" ")

        val whitespaceBeforeBlock = block.prevLeaf(includeEmpty = true)
        assertThat(whitespaceBeforeBlock?.elementType).isEqualTo(WHITE_SPACE)
        assertThat(whitespaceBeforeBlock?.text).isEqualTo(" ")
        assertThat(block.firstChildNode.elementType).isEqualTo(LBRACE)
        assertThat(root.text).isEqualTo("fun foo() {}")
    }

    private fun transformCodeToAST(code: String): FileASTNode =
        KtLintRuleEngine(
            ruleProviders =
                setOf(
                    RuleProvider { DummyRule() },
                ),
        ).transformToAst(
            Code.fromSnippet(code),
        )

    /**
     * A dummy rule for testing.
     */
    private class DummyRule : Rule(
        ruleId = RuleId("test:dummy-rule"),
        about = About(),
    ) {
        override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
            node: ASTNode,
            autoCorrect: Boolean,
            emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
        ) {
        }
    }
}

content.text:
Implemented a fix in `ASTNode.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe/AfterMe` to ensure whitespace inserted between two adjacent subtrees is added at their common parent (instead of incorrectly ending up inside the next/previous subtree, e.g. as the first child of a `{ ... }` block). If a whitespace already exists adjacent in leaf-order but is located in the wrong subtree, it is now relocated to the correct position.

Added a new unit test file `ASTNodeExtensionWhitespaceInsertionTest.kt` covering:
- inserting whitespace after `)` (RPAR) before a function body block
- inserting whitespace before `{` (LBRACE) of a function body block
- relocating incorrectly-inserted whitespace from inside the block to before the block in the parent AST

This prevents the “3 consecutive runs” formatter warning caused by repeated “autocorrect” attempts due to whitespace being inserted at an unexpected AST location.

 53→public class FunctionSignatureRule :
 54→    StandardRule(
 55→        id = "function-signature",
 56→        visitorModifiers =
 57→            setOf(
 58→                // Run after wrapping and spacing rules
 59→                VisitorModifier.RunAsLateAsPossible,
 60→            ),
 61→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 62→            setOf(
 63→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 64→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 65→                MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY,
 66→                FORCE_MULTILINE_WHEN_PARAMETER_COUNT_GREATER_OR_EQUAL_THAN_PROPERTY,
 67→                FUNCTION_BODY_EXPRESSION_WRAPPING_PROPERTY,
 68→            ),
 69→    ),
 70→    Rule.Experimental {
 71→    private var codeStyle = CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 72→    private var indentConfig = DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 73→    private var maxLineLength = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 74→    private var functionSignatureWrappingMinimumParameters =
 75→        FORCE_MULTILINE_WHEN_PARAMETER_COUNT_GREATER_OR_EQUAL_THAN_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 76→    private var functionBodyExpressionWrapping = FUNCTION_BODY_EXPRESSION_WRAPPING_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 77→
 78→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 79→        codeStyle = editorConfig[CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY]
 80→        functionSignatureWrappingMinimumParameters = editorConfig[FORCE_MULTILINE_WHEN_PARAMETER_COUNT_GREATER_OR_EQUAL_THAN_PROPERTY]
 81→        functionBodyExpressionWrapping = editorConfig[FUNCTION_BODY_EXPRESSION_WRAPPING_PROPERTY]
 82→        indentConfig =
 83→            IndentConfig(
 84→                indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 85→                tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 86→            )
 87→        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
 88→    }
 89→
 90→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 91→        node: ASTNode,
 92→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 93→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 94→    ) {
 95→        if (node.elementType == FUN) {
 96→            node
 97→                .functionSignatureNodes()
 98→                .any { it.elementType == EOL_COMMENT || it.elementType == BLOCK_COMMENT }
 99→                .ifTrue {
100→                    // Rewriting function signatures in a consistent manner is hard or sometimes even impossible. For
101→                    // example a multiline signature which could fit on one line can not be rewritten in case it
102→                    // contains an EOL comment. Rewriting a single line signature which exceeds the max line length to a
103→                    // multiline signature is hard when it contains block comments. For now, it does not seem worth the
104→                    // effort to attempt it.
105→                    return
106→                }
107→
108→            visitFunctionSignature(node, emit, autoCorrect)
109→        }
110→    }
111→
112→    private fun ASTNode.functionSignatureNodes(): List<ASTNode> {
113→        // Find the signature including the element that has to be placed on the same line as the function signature
114→        //     fun foo(bar: String) {
115→        // or
116→        //     fun foo(bar: String) =
117→        val firstCodeChild = getFirstCodeChild()
118→        val startOfBodyBlock =
119→            this
120→                .findChildByType(BLOCK)
121→                ?.firstChildNode
122→        val startOfBodyExpression = this.findChildByType(EQ)
123→        return collectLeavesRecursively()
124→            .childrenBetween(
125→                startASTNodePredicate = { it == firstCodeChild },
126→                endASTNodePredicate = { it == startOfBodyBlock || it == startOfBodyExpression },
127→            )
128→    }
129→
130→    private fun ASTNode.getFirstCodeChild(): ASTNode? {
131→        val funNode =
132→            if (elementType == FUN_KEYWORD) {
133→                this.treeParent
134→            } else {
135→                this
136→            }
137→        funNode
138→            ?.findChildByType(MODIFIER_LIST)
139→            ?.let { modifierList ->
140→                val iterator = modifierList.children().iterator()
141→                var currentNode: ASTNode
142→                while (iterator.hasNext()) {
143→                    currentNode = iterator.next()
144→                    if (currentNode.elementType != ANNOTATION &&
145→                        currentNode.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY &&
146→                        currentNode.elementType != WHITE_SPACE
147→                    ) {
148→                        return currentNode
149→                    }
150→                }
151→                return modifierList.nextCodeSibling()
152→            }
153→        return funNode.nextCodeLeaf()
154→    }

156→    private fun visitFunctionSignature(
157→        node: ASTNode,
158→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
159→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
160→    ) {
161→        require(node.elementType == FUN)
162→
163→        val forceMultilineSignature =
164→            node.hasMinimumNumberOfParameters() ||
165→                node.containsMultilineParameter() ||
166→                (codeStyle == ktlint_official && node.containsAnnotatedParameter())
167→        if (isMaxLineLengthSet()) {
168→            val singleLineFunctionSignatureLength = calculateFunctionSignatureLengthAsSingleLineSignature(node, emit, autoCorrect)
169→            // Function signatures not having parameters, should not be reformatted automatically. It would result in function signatures
170→            // like below, which are not acceptable:
171→            //     fun aVeryLongFunctionName(
172→            //     ) = "some-value"
173→            //
174→            //     fun aVeryLongFunctionName(
175→            //     ): SomeVeryLongTypeName =
176→            //         SomeVeryLongTypeName(...)
177→            // Leave it up to the max-line-length rule to detect those violations so that the developer can handle it manually.
178→            val rewriteFunctionSignatureWithParameters = node.countParameters() > 0 && singleLineFunctionSignatureLength > maxLineLength
179→            if (forceMultilineSignature || rewriteFunctionSignatureWithParameters) {
180→                fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline = true, dryRun = false)
181→                // Due to rewriting the function signature, the remaining length on the last line of the multiline
182→                // signature needs to be recalculated
183→                val lengthOfLastLine = recalculateRemainLengthForFirstLineOfBodyExpression(node)
184→                fixFunctionBody(node, emit, autoCorrect, maxLineLength - lengthOfLastLine)
185→            } else {
186→                fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline = false, dryRun = false)
187→                fixFunctionBody(node, emit, autoCorrect, maxLineLength - singleLineFunctionSignatureLength)
188→            }
189→        } else {
190→            // When max line length is not set then keep it as single line function signature only when the original
191→            // signature already was a single line signature. Otherwise, rewrite the entire signature as a multiline
192→            // signature.
193→            val rewriteToSingleLineFunctionSignature =
194→                node
195→                    .functionSignatureNodes()
196→                    .none { it.textContains('\n') }
197→            if (!forceMultilineSignature && rewriteToSingleLineFunctionSignature) {
198→                fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline = false, dryRun = false)
199→            } else {
200→                fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline = true, dryRun = false)
201→            }
202→        }
203→    }
204→
205→    private fun recalculateRemainLengthForFirstLineOfBodyExpression(node: ASTNode): Int {
206→        val closingParenthesis =
207→            node
208→                .findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST)
209→                ?.findChildByType(RPAR)
210→        val tailNodesOfFunctionSignature =
211→            node
212→                .functionSignatureNodes()
213→                .childrenBetween(
214→                    startASTNodePredicate = { it == closingParenthesis },
215→                    endASTNodePredicate = { false },
216→                )
217→
218→        return node.indent(false).length +
219→            tailNodesOfFunctionSignature.sumOf { it.text.length }
220→    }
221→
222→    private fun ASTNode.containsMultilineParameter(): Boolean =
223→        findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST)
224→            ?.children()
225→            .orEmpty()
226→            .filter { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
227→            .any { it.textContains('\n') }
228→
229→    private fun ASTNode.containsAnnotatedParameter(): Boolean =
230→        findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST)
231→            ?.children()
232→            .orEmpty()
233→            .filter { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
234→            .any { it.isAnnotated() }
235→
236→    private fun ASTNode.isAnnotated() =
237→        findChildByType(MODIFIER_LIST)
238→            ?.children()
239→            .orEmpty()
240→            .any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }

242→    private fun calculateFunctionSignatureLengthAsSingleLineSignature(
243→        node: ASTNode,
244→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
245→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
246→    ): Int {
247→        val actualFunctionSignatureLength = node.getFunctionSignatureLength()
248→
249→        // Calculate the length of the function signature in case it would be rewritten as single line (and without a
250→        // maximum line length). The white space correction will be calculated via a dry run of the actual fix.
251→        return actualFunctionSignatureLength +
252→            // Calculate the white space correction in case the signature would be rewritten to a single line
253→            fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline = false, dryRun = true)
254→    }
255→
256→    private fun ASTNode.getFunctionSignatureLength() = indent(false).length + getFunctionSignatureNodesLength()
257→
258→    private fun ASTNode.getFunctionSignatureNodesLength() =
259→        functionSignatureNodes()
260→            .joinTextToString()
261→            .length
262→
263→    private fun fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(
264→        node: ASTNode,
265→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
266→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
267→        multiline: Boolean,
268→        dryRun: Boolean,
269→    ): Int {
270→        var whiteSpaceCorrection = 0
271→
272→        val valueParameterList = requireNotNull(node.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST))
273→        val firstParameterInList =
274→            valueParameterList
275→                .children()
276→                .firstOrNull { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
277→
278→        whiteSpaceCorrection +=
279→            if (firstParameterInList == null) {
280→                // handle empty parameter list
281→                fixWhiteSpacesInEmptyValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, dryRun)
282→            } else {
283→                fixWhiteSpacesBeforeFirstParameterInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline, dryRun) +
284→                    fixWhiteSpacesBetweenParametersInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline, dryRun) +
285→                    fixWhiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline, dryRun)
286→            }
287→
288→        return whiteSpaceCorrection
289→    }
290→
291→    private fun fixWhiteSpacesInEmptyValueParameterList(
292→        node: ASTNode,
293→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
294→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
295→        dryRun: Boolean,
296→    ): Int {
297→        var whiteSpaceCorrection = 0
298→
299→        val valueParameterList = requireNotNull(node.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST))
300→
301→        valueParameterList
302→            .children()
303→            .filter { it.elementType != LPAR && it.elementType != RPAR }
304→            .also { elementsInValueParameterList ->
305→                // Functions with comments in the value parameter list are excluded from processing before. So an "empty" value
306→                // parameter list should only contain a single whitespace element
307→                require(elementsInValueParameterList.count() <= 1)
308→            }
309→            .firstOrNull()
310→            ?.let { whiteSpace ->
311→                if (!dryRun) {
312→                    emit(
313→                        whiteSpace.startOffset,
314→                        "No whitespace expected in empty parameter list",
315→                        true,
316→                    )
317→                }
318→                if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
319→                    whiteSpace.treeParent.removeChild(whiteSpace)
320→                } else {
321→                    whiteSpaceCorrection -= whiteSpace.textLength
322→                }
323→            }
324→
325→        return whiteSpaceCorrection
326→    }
327→
328→    private fun fixWhiteSpacesBeforeFirstParameterInValueParameterList(
329→        node: ASTNode,
330→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
331→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
332→        multiline: Boolean,
333→        dryRun: Boolean,
334→    ): Int {
335→        var whiteSpaceCorrection = 0
336→
337→        val valueParameterList = requireNotNull(node.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST))
338→        val firstParameterInList =
339→            valueParameterList
340→                .children()
341→                .first { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
342→
343→        val firstParameter = firstParameterInList.firstChildNode
344→        firstParameter
345→            ?.prevLeaf()
346→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE }
347→            .let { whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier ->
348→                if (multiline) {
349→                    val expectedParameterIndent = indentConfig.childIndentOf(node)
350→                    if (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier == null ||
351→                        whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier.text != expectedParameterIndent
352→                    ) {
353→                        if (!dryRun) {
354→                            emit(
355→                                firstParameterInList.startOffset,
356→                                "Newline expected after opening parenthesis",
357→                                true,
358→                            )
359→                        }
360→                        if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
361→                            valueParameterList.firstChildNode.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(expectedParameterIndent)
362→                        } else {
363→                            whiteSpaceCorrection += expectedParameterIndent.length - (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier?.textLength ?: 0)
364→                        }
365→                    }
366→                } else {
367→                    if (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier != null) {
368→                        if (!dryRun) {
369→                            emit(
370→                                firstParameter!!.startOffset,
371→                                "No whitespace expected between opening parenthesis and first parameter name",
372→                                true,
373→                            )
374→                        }
375→                        if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
376→                            whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier.treeParent.removeChild(whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier)
377→                        } else {
378→                            whiteSpaceCorrection -= whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier.textLength
379→                        }
380→                    }
381→                }
382→            }
383→
384→        return whiteSpaceCorrection
385→    }
386→
387→    private fun fixWhiteSpacesBetweenParametersInValueParameterList(
388→        node: ASTNode,
389→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
390→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
391→        multiline: Boolean,
392→        dryRun: Boolean,
393→    ): Int {
394→        var whiteSpaceCorrection = 0
395→
396→        val valueParameterList = requireNotNull(node.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST))
397→        val firstParameterInList =
398→            valueParameterList
399→                .children()
400→                .first { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
401→
402→        valueParameterList
403→            .children()
404→            .filter { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
405→            .filter { it != firstParameterInList }
406→            .forEach { valueParameter ->
407→                val firstChildNodeInValueParameter = valueParameter.firstChildNode
408→                firstChildNodeInValueParameter
409→                    ?.prevLeaf()
410→                    ?.takeIf { it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE }
411→                    .let { whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier ->
412→                        if (multiline) {
413→                            val expectedParameterIndent = indentConfig.childIndentOf(node)
414→                            if (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier == null ||
415→                                whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier.text != expectedParameterIndent
416→                            ) {
417→                                if (!dryRun) {
418→                                    emit(
419→                                        valueParameter.startOffset,
420→                                        "Parameter should start on a newline",
421→                                        true,
422→                                    )
423→                                }
424→                                if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
425→                                    firstChildNodeInValueParameter.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(expectedParameterIndent)
426→                                } else {
427→                                    whiteSpaceCorrection += expectedParameterIndent.length - (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier?.textLength ?: 0)
428→                                }
429→                            }
430→                        } else {
431→                            if (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier == null || whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier.text != " ") {
432→                                if (!dryRun) {
433→                                    emit(
434→                                        firstChildNodeInValueParameter!!.startOffset,
435→                                        "Single whitespace expected before parameter",
436→                                        true,
437→                                    )
438→                                }
439→                                if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
440→                                    firstChildNodeInValueParameter.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(" ")
441→                                } else {
442→                                    whiteSpaceCorrection += 1 - (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier?.textLength ?: 0)
443→                                }
444→                            }
445→                        }
446→                    }
447→            }
448→
449→        return whiteSpaceCorrection
450→    }

452→    private fun fixWhiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis(
453→        node: ASTNode,
454→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
455→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
456→        multiline: Boolean,
457→        dryRun: Boolean,
458→    ): Int {
459→        var whiteSpaceCorrection = 0
460→
461→        val newlineAndIndent = node.indent()
462→        val valueParameterList = requireNotNull(node.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST))
463→
464→        val closingParenthesis = valueParameterList.findChildByType(RPAR)
465→        closingParenthesis
466→            ?.prevSibling()
467→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE }
468→            .let { whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis ->
469→                if (multiline) {
470→                    if (whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis == null ||
471→                        whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis.text != newlineAndIndent
472→                    ) {
473→                        if (!dryRun) {
474→                            emit(
475→                                closingParenthesis!!.startOffset,
476→                                "Newline expected before closing parenthesis",
477→                                true,
478→                            )
479→                        }
480→                        if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
481→                            closingParenthesis!!.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(newlineAndIndent)
482→                        } else {
483→                            whiteSpaceCorrection += newlineAndIndent.length - (whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis?.textLength ?: 0)
484→                        }
485→                    }
486→                } else {
487→                    if (whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis != null &&
488→                        whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis.nextLeaf()?.elementType == RPAR
489→                    ) {
490→                        if (!dryRun) {
491→                            emit(
492→                                whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis.startOffset,
493→                                "No whitespace expected between last parameter and closing parenthesis",
494→                                true,
495→                            )
496→                        }
497→                        if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
498→                            whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis.treeParent.removeChild(whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis)
499→                        } else {
500→                            whiteSpaceCorrection -= whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis.textLength
501→                        }
502→                    }
503→                }
504→            }
505→        return whiteSpaceCorrection
506→    }

508→    private fun fixFunctionBody(
509→        node: ASTNode,
510→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
511→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
512→        maxLengthRemainingForFirstLineOfBodyExpression: Int,
513→    ) {
514→        if (node.findChildByType(EQ) == null) {
515→            fixFunctionBodyBlock(node, emit, autoCorrect)
516→        } else {
517→            fixFunctionBodyExpression(node, emit, autoCorrect, maxLengthRemainingForFirstLineOfBodyExpression)
518→        }
519→    }
520→
521→    private fun fixFunctionBodyExpression(
522→        node: ASTNode,
523→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
524→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
525→        maxLengthRemainingForFirstLineOfBodyExpression: Int,
526→    ) {
527→        val lastNodeOfFunctionSignatureWithBodyExpression =
528→            node
529→                .findChildByType(EQ)
530→                ?.nextLeaf(includeEmpty = true)
531→                ?: return
532→        val bodyNodes = node.getFunctionBody(lastNodeOfFunctionSignatureWithBodyExpression)
533→        val whiteSpaceBeforeFunctionBodyExpression = bodyNodes.getStartingWhitespaceOrNull()
534→        val functionBodyExpressionNodes = bodyNodes.dropWhile { it.isWhiteSpace() }
535→
536→        val functionBodyExpressionLines =
537→            functionBodyExpressionNodes
538→                .joinTextToString()
539→                .split("\n")
540→        functionBodyExpressionLines
541→            .firstOrNull()
542→            ?.also { firstLineOfBodyExpression ->
543→                if (whiteSpaceBeforeFunctionBodyExpression.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
544→                    lastNodeOfFunctionSignatureWithBodyExpression
545→                        .nextCodeSibling()
546→                        .takeIf { it?.elementType == ANNOTATED_EXPRESSION }
547→                        ?.let {
548→                            // Never merge an annotated expression body with function signature as this conflicts with the Annotation rule
549→                            return
550→                        }
551→                    val mergeWithFunctionSignature =
552→                        if (firstLineOfBodyExpression.length < maxLengthRemainingForFirstLineOfBodyExpression) {
553→                            functionBodyExpressionWrapping == default ||
554→                                (functionBodyExpressionWrapping == multiline && functionBodyExpressionLines.size == 1) ||
555→                                node.isMultilineFunctionSignatureWithoutExplicitReturnType(
556→                                    lastNodeOfFunctionSignatureWithBodyExpression,
557→                                )
558→                        } else {
559→                            false
560→                        }
561→                    if (mergeWithFunctionSignature) {
562→                        emit(
563→                            whiteSpaceBeforeFunctionBodyExpression!!.startOffset,
564→                            "First line of body expression fits on same line as function signature",
565→                            true,
566→                        )
567→                        if (autoCorrect) {
568→                            (whiteSpaceBeforeFunctionBodyExpression as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(" ")
569→                        }
570→                    }
571→                } else if (whiteSpaceBeforeFunctionBodyExpression == null ||
572→                    !whiteSpaceBeforeFunctionBodyExpression.textContains('\n')
573→                ) {
574→                    if (node.isMultilineFunctionSignatureWithoutExplicitReturnType(lastNodeOfFunctionSignatureWithBodyExpression) &&
575→                        firstLineOfBodyExpression.length + 1 <= maxLengthRemainingForFirstLineOfBodyExpression
576→                    ) {
577→                        if (whiteSpaceBeforeFunctionBodyExpression == null ||
578→                            whiteSpaceBeforeFunctionBodyExpression.text != " "
579→                        ) {
580→                            emit(
581→                                functionBodyExpressionNodes.first().startOffset,
582→                                "Single whitespace expected before expression body",
583→                                true,
584→                            )
585→                            if (autoCorrect) {
586→                                functionBodyExpressionNodes
587→                                    .first()
588→                                    .upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(" ")
589→                            }
590→                        }
591→                    } else if (firstLineOfBodyExpression.length + 1 > maxLengthRemainingForFirstLineOfBodyExpression ||
592→                        (functionBodyExpressionWrapping == multiline && functionBodyExpressionLines.size > 1) ||
593→                        functionBodyExpressionWrapping == always
594→                    ) {
595→                        emit(
596→                            functionBodyExpressionNodes.first().startOffset,
597→                            "Newline expected before expression body",
598→                            true,
599→                        )
600→                        if (autoCorrect) {
601→                            functionBodyExpressionNodes
602→                                .first()

682→    private fun List<ASTNode>.childrenBetween(
683→        startASTNodePredicate: (ASTNode) -> Boolean = { _ -> true },
684→        endASTNodePredicate: (ASTNode) -> Boolean = { _ -> false },
685→    ): List<ASTNode> {
686→        val iterator = iterator()
687→        var currentNode: ASTNode
688→        val childrenBetween: MutableList<ASTNode> = mutableListOf()
689→
690→        while (iterator.hasNext()) {
691→            currentNode = iterator.next()
692→            if (startASTNodePredicate(currentNode)) {
693→                childrenBetween.add(currentNode)
694→                break
695→            }
696→        }
697→
698→        while (iterator.hasNext()) {
699→            currentNode = iterator.next()
700→            childrenBetween.add(currentNode)
701→            if (endASTNodePredicate(currentNode)) {
702→                break
703→            }
704→        }
705→
706→        return childrenBetween
707→    }
